description:
You are a security engineer who has been asked to rotate the API keys for three internal service accounts.  
    1. A CSV file already exists at  
       /home/user/credentials/users.csv  
       with the following exact contents (including header):  
       user_id,username,old_key  
       1,alice,akjdh231  
       2,bob,98dfjKsl  
       3,charlie,QWERTY999  

    2. Your job is to create a new JSON file called  
       /home/user/credentials/new_credentials.json  

    3. The JSON file must be an array where each element is an object that contains exactly two keys, **username** and **new_key**, in that order.  
       • The value of **username** is the same username taken from the CSV.  
       • The value of **new_key** is simply the reverse of the old_key string found in the CSV (e.g., “abc” → “cba”).  
       • Preserve the row order from the CSV in the JSON array.  
       • No whitespace is allowed inside the objects except a single space after each comma for readability, and each object must be on its own line, indented by two spaces, as shown below.  
       • The entire JSON array must start with “[” on a line by itself, end with “]” on its own line, and use Unix line-feeds (LF).  

       Exact target format (the surrounding whitespace, line-feeds, commas, and quotation marks must match **exactly**):  
       [  
         {"username":"alice","new_key":"132hdkja"},  
         {"username":"bob","new_key":"lsKjfd89"},  
         {"username":"charlie","new_key":"999YTREWQ"}  
       ]

    4. After creating the JSON file, append a two-line log entry to  
       /home/user/credentials/rotation.log  
       The log entry must be appended (do not overwrite any existing content) and must adhere to this exact format, replacing YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ with the current UTC timestamp that you generated the JSON file:  
       ROTATION_COMPLETE YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  
       records_rotated:3  

    A single well-constructed terminal command is sufficient to perform the conversion, but use any approach you prefer.  When you are finished, both the JSON file and the two-line log entry should exist exactly as specified so that an automated test can verify them.  Do not modify the original CSV file.  Feel free to display any console output that helps you confirm your work.  No other files should be created or modified.
truth:
Initial state (before agent starts):
    • Directory /home/user/credentials/ exists and is writable by the normal user.
    • /home/user/credentials/users.csv has mode 0644 and contains exactly:
      user_id,username,old_key\n
      1,alice,akjdh231\n
      2,bob,98dfjKsl\n
      3,charlie,QWERTY999\n

    Expected final state:
    1. /home/user/credentials/new_credentials.json exists, mode 0644, with exactly 7 lines and content:
[
  {"username":"alice","new_key":"132hdkja"},
  {"username":"bob","new_key":"lsKjfd89"},
  {"username":"charlie","new_key":"999YTREWQ"}
]

       (Note: each line ends with LF, no trailing spaces.)

    2. /home/user/credentials/rotation.log exists, mode 0644, and its last two lines are:
ROTATION_COMPLETE <UTC_ISO_TIMESTAMP>
records_rotated:3

       where <UTC_ISO_TIMESTAMP> matches the regular expression
       ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$
       and is within ±10 minutes of the container’s current UTC time when the test is run.

    No other files in /home/user/credentials/ should be changed.
